mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative or flat 20-day S&P 500 returns during mid-cycle expansion periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL exceeding 190.0 signals entrenched inflation that forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 190.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline shows a negative or insignificant coefficient in a 60-day forward return regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, directly supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates exhibit a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns across a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden supply-side contraction or demand shock, triggering immediate market repricing and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following an INDPRO decline greater than 0.5%.
